context: Tests whether the agent builds a direct scoring evaluation pipeline following the correct 5-layer architecture, uses the right statistical metrics for each evaluation type, includes input validation with weight normalization, uses temperature 0.3 for LLM calls, and produces a properly structured output schema.

checklist.name: Five pipeline layers
checklist.description: The implementation includes or describes all five layers: Criteria Loader, Primary Scorer, Bias Mitigation, Confidence Scoring, and Output formatting — not just a direct LLM call

checklist.name: Criteria loaded from config
checklist.description: Criteria are loaded from a configuration file or external source (e.g., JSON/YAML file), not hardcoded strings inside the scoring logic

checklist.name: Input validation present
checklist.description: The pipeline validates inputs before processing: checks that response is non-empty, prompt is non-empty, and at least one criterion is provided

checklist.name: Weight normalization
checklist.description: Criterion weights are normalized to sum to 1.0 (either enforced by validation or calculated automatically)

checklist.name: Justification before score in prompt
checklist.description: The direct scoring prompt instructs the LLM judge to find evidence and justify first, then assign a score — not score first

checklist.name: Temperature 0.3
checklist.description: The LLM call for scoring uses temperature 0.3 (or documents/recommends this value for consistency)

checklist.name: Structured output schema
checklist.description: The output includes a structured result with at minimum: success flag, per-criterion scores, justifications/evidence, and a weighted_score or overall_score field

checklist.name: Correct metric for ordinal scale
checklist.description: When discussing or implementing validation against human judgments on ordinal (1-5) scores, uses Spearman's rho or Kendall's tau — NOT just raw agreement percentage

checklist.name: Correct metric for binary task
checklist.description: When discussing or implementing pass/fail classification metrics, uses Precision, Recall, F1, or Cohen's kappa — NOT just accuracy

checklist.name: Systematic disagreement emphasis
checklist.description: The evaluation report or metrics section tracks per-criterion disagreement patterns (or notes that systematic per-criterion errors are more concerning than overall noise)

checklist.name: Confidence includes evidence factor
checklist.description: The confidence calibration logic accounts for evidence count or evidence strength — not just position consistency alone

checklist.name: One criterion one aspect
checklist.description: Each criterion in the sample rubric/config measures exactly one aspect — no criterion bundles multiple concerns into a single score
